sglang.title: Enable unit tests for AMD CI.

This PR begins to enable the 1 gpu, 2 gpu, and 8 gpu unit tests on AMD CI, starting with deepseek, mla, and moe tests.
Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Code Formatting with Pre-Commit.
 Add unit tests as outlined in the Running Unit Tests.
 Update documentation

sglang.title: [fix][RL] Remove the incorrect barrier in init_weights_update_group
sglang.author: zhuzilin
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Modifications

This PR fixed the init_weights_update_group by removing an incorrect barrier. The reason for the bug is that when doing rl training with custom resourse management (e.g. using ray), each engine will not have access to a device rank.
Thank you for your time on reviewing thi

vllm.title: [Model] GritLM supports other attention backends
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR works around the issue where the xformers attention backend fails to be selected in GritLM tests, by simply supporting other attention backends for this model as well.

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Simpler, and so that connector impls don't need to explicitly define/implement a type for this. We could still keep the type and just use it inside the nixl connector...

vllm.title: [Doc] Update prefix cache metrics to counting tokens
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #18003 changes prefix caching metric from counting blocks to counting tokens. This PR updates the doc to sync with that PR.

vllm.title: [V1][Spec Decode] Share input embedding of target model with EAGLE draft model to free ~1GB for llama 3 model

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR:

Fixes: #16035 (comment)
Frees ~1GB GPU memory for llama 3 model
Add output logging for sanity check in eagle offline bench
Tested by using my hacky metric PR and ensuring AL is high.

vllm.title: Modularize fused experts and integrate PPLX kernels
vllm.author: bnellnm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR defines a set of base classes used to make MoE kernels more modular. The goal is to be able to utilize different communication mechanisms with any fused MoE kernel without needing to have combinatoric implementations.
The fused moe kernels are broken down into the following components:
[Rout

For MLA we end up passing tensor slices to the rotary embeddings, like:
        q[..., self.qk_nope_head_dim:], k_pe = self.rotary_emb(

make the kernel natural support this without . contiguous calls

vllm.title: [V1] Structured Outputs + Thinking compatibility
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR brings thinking support to structured outputs in V1. Currently, if you want to use thinking parser in conjunction with structured outputs, you have to use the V0 engine.
This is also compatible with speculative decoding
This PR also refactor the tokenizer onto the structured_output_manager i

initializing its zmq socket in a new thread, which gives a window of
opportunity for the subscriber crea

vllm.title: [V1] Support multiple kv connectors
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Co-authored with @njhill.
We take advantage of the kv_connector_extra_config: dict[str, Any] already present in KVTransferConfig to stash all the connectors we want in an ordered list of kwargs. The new MultiConnector then creates a connector from each item in its list of KVTransferConfig.kv_connect

This change makes most of the parameterized tests in pytest tests/entrypoints/openai/test_openai_schema.py pass. We handle user input errors, enrich HTTP error messages with more information, and update the error response definitions for several API endpoints.
The remaining tests that fail are POST

vllm.title: [v1] Support multiple KV cache groups in GPU model runner
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Should be merged after #17483
This PR finishes the hybrid allocator support on worker side. It does the following things:

change block_ids in SchedulerOutput to list[list[int]], where the outer list is for multiple kv cache groups and inner list is for blocks in one group.
Create BlockTable class f

vllm.title: [V1][Metrics] Remove unused code
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This framework was added in #10907 but we never ended up using it.

vllm.title: [Chore] astral's ty
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
https://github.com/astral-sh/ty
This PR adds initial support for ty, newest type checker on the block (ty is so fast, so much faster than mypy)
We should then migrate from mypy to ty gradually once it becomes more stable. For now this configuration helps with LSP-related features.
Signed-off-by: Aar

vllm.title: [Fix][ROCm] Enforce eager for all encoder-decoder models on ROCm
vllm.author: ProExpertProg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Turns out CUDAGraph capture is not yet supported for encoder-decoder models on AMD. #18104 removed the assert but now it produces garbage.
This produces a memory fault on main, works (with enforce-eager disabled and correct output) with this PR.
python examples/offline_inference/basic/generate.py --

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix fp8 tests for triton_unified_attention for Triton 3.3
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The FP8 unit tests for triton_unified_attention don't pass for Triton 3.3.
We didn't catch this through CI when upgrading Triton because the corresponding file hadn't been moved into the new "kernels" subfolder.
This PR resolves both issues.
cc @LucasWilkinson

Support custom implementations of VideoLoader backends.

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
While browsing this code, I noticed this code wasn't using the
make_zmq_socket helper from vllm.utils. Update it to use it.
The benefits are:

this fixes IPv6 support
Optimize buffer sizes based on system memory to improve throughput,
though this part doesn't seem too relevant for how the current so
